VAL-DB-001.evidence: \d users shows: id VARCHAR(64) PK, display_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, kms_key_arn VARCHAR(256) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). Primary key users_pkey on id. Referenced by health_data, oura_connections, share_grants FKs with ON DELETE CASCADE.
VAL-DB-001.details: All columns present with correct types, constraints, and defaults.

VAL-DB-002.evidence: \d oura_connections shows: id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL FK->users ON DELETE CASCADE, access_token_enc BYTEA NOT NULL, refresh_token_enc BYTEA NOT NULL, token_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, last_sync_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, sync_cursor VARCHAR(256) nullable, sync_status VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'idle', sync_error TEXT nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). UNIQUE constraint uq_oura_connections_user on user_id. CHECK constraint chk_oura_sync_status ensuring sync_status IN ('idle','syncing','error').
VAL-DB-002.details: All columns, encrypted token fields (BYTEA), sync_status CHECK constraint, and UNIQUE on user_id confirmed.

VAL-DB-003.evidence: \d health_data shows: id BIGSERIAL (bigint with nextval), user_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL FK->users ON DELETE CASCADE, metric_type VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL, date DATE NOT NULL, value_encrypted BYTEA NOT NULL, source VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL, source_id VARCHAR(256) nullable, imported_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). UNIQUE constraint uq_health_data_user_metric_date_source on (user_id, metric_type, date, source). Additional indexes on (user_id, metric_type, date) and (user_id, metric_type).
VAL-DB-003.details: BIGSERIAL id, user_id FK, metric_type, date, value_encrypted (BYTEA), source, and UNIQUE(user_id, metric_type, date, source) all confirmed.

VAL-DB-004.evidence: \d share_grants shows: id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), token VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL UNIQUE, owner_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL FK->users ON DELETE CASCADE, label VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, note VARCHAR(1000) nullable, allowed_metrics TEXT[] NOT NULL, data_start DATE NOT NULL, data_end DATE NOT NULL, grant_expires TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, view_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, last_viewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). CHECK constraints: chk_share_grants_date_range (data_end >= data_start), chk_share_grants_metrics_nonempty (array_length > 0). Partial index idx_share_grants_active_token on token WHERE revoked_at IS NULL.
VAL-DB-004.details: All columns including allowed_metrics (TEXT[]), CHECK constraints on dates and non-empty metrics, and partial index on active tokens confirmed.

VAL-DB-005.evidence: \d audit_events shows: id BIGSERIAL (bigint with nextval), owner_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL, actor_type VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL, actor_id VARCHAR(64) nullable, grant_id UUID nullable, event_type VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL, resource_type VARCHAR(64) nullable, resource_detail JSONB nullable, ip_address INET nullable, user_agent TEXT nullable, session_id VARCHAR(256) nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). Indexes: idx_audit_events_owner_created on (owner_id, created_at), idx_audit_events_owner_type_created on (owner_id, event_type, created_at), idx_audit_events_grant_created on (grant_id, created_at) WHERE grant_id IS NOT NULL. CHECK constraint chk_audit_actor_type. Trigger trg_audit_events_immutable prevents UPDATE/DELETE.
VAL-DB-005.details: BIGSERIAL id, all required columns, ip_address (INET), indexes on (owner_id, created_at) and more, plus immutability trigger confirmed.

VAL-DB-015.evidence: SELECT extname, extversion FROM pg_extension WHERE extname = 'pgcrypto' returned: pgcrypto 1.3. SELECT gen_random_uuid() returned: e8c7e24f-f4d7-47d9-b106-7fd44b0e4e9b (valid UUID).
VAL-DB-015.details: pgcrypto extension enabled (v1.3) and gen_random_uuid() is functional.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: all 6 passed. Users, oura_connections, health_data, share_grants, and audit_events tables all exist with correct schemas, constraints, indexes, and foreign keys. pgcrypto extension is enabled with gen_random_uuid() available.
